title: Whats Will Got To Do With It

jokes.text: Negotiating for a bigger royalty? / No, a better table.
jokes.setup: Carlton assumes Will's phone negotiation was about music business royalties
jokes.punchline: Will reveals he was just getting a better restaurant table
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection that shows Will's inexperience - thinks restaurant reservations are as important as business deals. Good economy and character work.

jokes.text: Hey, now I know how Hammer must have felt the week he was famous.
jokes.setup: Will celebrating his newfound power as a manager
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to MC Hammer's brief peak fame
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural reference that works on multiple levels - both about fame's fleeting nature and Hammer's actual career trajectory. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Like white folks watch Wings.
jokes.setup: Will explaining how closely Carlton will watch him as business manager
jokes.punchline: Comparison to white people's devotion to the sitcom Wings
jokes.explanation: Solid cultural observation about TV viewership demographics. Good economy but relies on stereotyping.

jokes.text: Yeah, Rob, Fab. Blame it on the rain, brothers.
jokes.setup: Will greeting record executives Rob and Fab
jokes.punchline: References Milli Vanilli's 'Blame It on the Rain' song
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference that fits Will's character but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: I'm having a bust of my head made. It's getting helicoptered in on Thursday.
jokes.setup: Will showing off his fancy office setup
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously over-the-top method of receiving a bust of himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Will's newfound ego and delusions of grandeur. The helicopter detail makes it absurdly specific.

jokes.text: Then blang, over to Phoenix. Then bap, bap, bap, bladow, over to Orlando. Then blip-bloop, we gonna slide up to Seattle.
jokes.setup: Will describing Ashley's tour schedule
jokes.punchline: Uses increasingly ridiculous sound effects instead of actual logistics
jokes.explanation: Pure Will Smith physical comedy through sound effects. Shows his style but goes on a bit long.

jokes.text: Oh, no, no, no, bro, I said 'I got dates.' See, I got Tina in New York. I got Beth in Seattle. Ohh. Actually, I might need a couple days in Seattle. Beth is a big girl, you know?
jokes.setup: Record executives think Will has booked concert dates
jokes.punchline: He's talking about romantic dates with women, including a crude comment about Beth's size
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection but the weight joke feels cheap and mean-spirited, bringing down the overall quality.

jokes.text: Absolutely. Most of the arcades are losing money.
jokes.setup: Will says video is dead, Carlton agrees
jokes.punchline: Carlton thinks they're talking about video games, not music videos
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - earnestly helpful but completely missing the point. Great economy and character work.

jokes.text: We gonna set up lunch with her and Tevin Campbell. Midway through, she gonna stand up, take a drink and splash it in his face. Right? Then she slap him, call him a punk... Then we plant a story that Tevin been sleeping outside her crib with a rifle.
jokes.setup: Will proposing a publicity stunt
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate and harmful fake scandal involving stalking
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Will's inexperience, but the fake violence angle makes it somewhat uncomfortable rather than purely funny.

jokes.text: Then you can be there for him.
jokes.setup: Gordy reveals Tevin Campbell is his godson after Will's elaborate scheme to frame him
jokes.punchline: Will's casual suggestion that Gordy can comfort Tevin after ruining his career
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly deadpan response that shows Will's complete lack of awareness about how terrible his plan is. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: That went great.
jokes.setup: The meeting clearly went horribly with the executives leaving uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: Will's complete misreading of the situation
jokes.explanation: Classic oblivious character moment. Simple but effective at showing Will's complete lack of self-awareness.

jokes.text: I was with Tisha last night. I can't walk that fast.
jokes.setup: Carlton wants Will to walk faster
jokes.punchline: Will implies he's sore from sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Standard sex joke that doesn't add much beyond showing Will's braggadocious character. Functional but unoriginal.

jokes.text: Who the hell are all these people?
jokes.setup: Will arrives late expecting to be in charge
jokes.punchline: Discovers a full recording session happening without him
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Will's comeuppance. His confusion is funny given his earlier confidence about running everything.

jokes.text: I'm just the man behind the man.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to reassure Will he's still important
jokes.punchline: Backhanded way of saying Will is being demoted
jokes.explanation: Standard Hollywood double-speak that works for the situation but isn't particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: Tito, Jermaine. Your pick.
jokes.setup: Jerry offering Will management of other clients
jokes.punchline: Offering him the least successful Jackson brothers
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference that shows how far Will has fallen - from hot new artist to managing has-beens. Efficient delivery.

jokes.text: This is better than television.
jokes.setup: Carlton eavesdropping on Will's dramatic confrontation
jokes.punchline: Comparing family drama to TV while literally being on television
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment that works both as Carlton being oblivious and the show acknowledging its own dramatic moments.

jokes.text: Oh, great. Now we'll never know how Will took the news.
jokes.setup: Will and Uncle Phil's argument goes to inaudible dialogue
jokes.punchline: Carlton treating it like a TV show that cut away at the wrong moment
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about TV conventions while staying true to Carlton's character as someone who treats real life like entertainment.

jokes.text: All this money for rent and I still got roaches.
jokes.setup: Will in his crappy apartment after stomping on roaches
jokes.punchline: Complaining about getting roaches despite paying rent
jokes.explanation: Standard complaint about apartment living. Shows Will's fall from grace but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Point? I have no...
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil says Will made his point
jokes.punchline: Will starts the same denial pattern but stops mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion of the pattern he just established. Shows Will realizing how ridiculous he sounds.

jokes.text: Well, gee, I could have sworn that Vivian and I had something to do with it.
jokes.setup: Will claims he 'made' Ashley Banks
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil dryly notes that he and Vivian literally made Ashley
jokes.explanation: Perfect Uncle Phil moment - using parental authority to deflate Will's grandiosity with dry humor.

jokes.text: Home? I have no home.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil mentions home again
jokes.punchline: Will falls back into the same dramatic denial
jokes.explanation: Running gag is getting repetitive by this point. The callback works but lacks the surprise of the earlier versions.

jokes.text: I got the worst luck with tenants.
jokes.setup: Landlord evicting Will
jokes.punchline: Complaining about tenant quality while kicking out someone for non-payment
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment for the landlord but fairly standard landlord joke territory.

jokes.text: It's so hard to smack you upside your head when you're not here.
jokes.setup: Will asking if Aunt Viv is happy to see him
jokes.punchline: She expresses affection through missing the ability to physically discipline him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Aunt Viv line - shows she cares by missing the ability to keep him in line. Good character work.

jokes.text: Gee, she hides it so well.
jokes.setup: Aunt Viv very obviously expressing her dislike for Ashley's new attitude
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically noting how 'subtle' she's being
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic reaction to obvious behavior. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Perhaps later on we could pour some water on her and watch her melt.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Ashley's bad attitude
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey comparing her to the Wicked Witch of the West
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey - sophisticated reference delivered with perfect butler disdain. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Don't just stand there, Ashley. Call him a good boy and toss him a treat.
jokes.setup: Carlton praising Ashley's success
jokes.punchline: Will comparing Carlton to a dog performing tricks
jokes.explanation: Good burn on Carlton's eagerness to please, but the dog comparison is somewhat mean-spirited.

jokes.text: Oh, really? So thanks. What the hell is my problem?
jokes.setup: Ashley saying she'll thank Will on her album cover
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic acceptance of this minimal recognition
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's frustration at being reduced to a footnote. Solid character moment but not particularly witty.

jokes.text: Right, just like you launched Ashley's career?
jokes.setup: Will pointing out that the talk show idea was his
jokes.punchline: Hilary dismissively comparing it to his 'successful' management career
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that shows Hilary can be cutting when she wants to be. Good economy and character work.

jokes.text: So young, so bitter.
jokes.setup: Will's sarcastic comment about 60 Minutes
jokes.punchline: Ashley's condescending dismissal treating Will like he's being childish
jokes.explanation: Good characterization of Ashley's new diva attitude - treating her older cousin like a child having a tantrum.

jokes.text: Somebody need to snatch her big helium head down out the stratosphere.
jokes.setup: Will complaining about Ashley's ego
jokes.punchline: Vivid metaphor comparing her inflated ego to a helium balloon
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor that captures Ashley's inflated ego perfectly. Very quotable and fits Will's colorful speech patterns.

jokes.text: I would have been happy paying you a token salary while someone else did the real work.
jokes.setup: Ashley claiming she didn't want to fire Will
jokes.punchline: Reveals she would have kept him as a figurehead while others did his job
jokes.explanation: Perfect showcase of Ashley's new callous attitude - the insult is wrapped in fake kindness, which makes it worse.

jokes.text: And, face it, you're small potatoes.
jokes.setup: Ashley explaining why she needs to move beyond Will
jokes.punchline: Directly calling him 'small potatoes'
jokes.explanation: The insult works for Ashley's character development but 'small potatoes' is a pretty cliched put-down.

jokes.text: I'll show you how small my potatoes are.
jokes.setup: Ashley just called him 'small potatoes'
jokes.punchline: Will's immediate threat to prove her wrong, using her exact phrasing
jokes.explanation: Great callback that shows Will's determination while using Ashley's own words against her. Good setup for his revenge plot.

jokes.text: The resemblance, much like the beat, goes on.
jokes.setup: Jazz noting that Will's new client looks like Ashley
jokes.punchline: Will making a pun on 'the beat goes on' about the resemblance continuing
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that works in context but not particularly memorable. Shows Will's cleverness but isn't his best wordplay.

jokes.text: Come on, Bashley.
jokes.setup: Will addressing his new Ashley look-alike client
jokes.punchline: Combining 'baby' and 'Ashley' into a pet name
jokes.explanation: Simple portmanteau that shows Will's plan but isn't particularly clever. Functional for the plot.

jokes.text: All I got was a promise she wouldn't press charges.
jokes.setup: Carlton complaining about being fired as business manager
jokes.punchline: Implies he was embezzling money
jokes.explanation: Great dark twist on Carlton's goody-two-shoes image - suggests he's actually corrupt

jokes.text: Just ask Kim Basinger.
jokes.setup: Carlton explaining that oral agreements are legally binding
jokes.punchline: Reference to Kim Basinger's legal troubles over breaking a verbal contract
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural reference that shows Carlton's legal knowledge and current events awareness. Good economy.

jokes.text: Man, that song is weak. / Last week.
jokes.setup: Will trying to give away Ashley's CD, kid calls it weak
jokes.punchline: Will agreeing and specifying it was only good last week
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of how quickly pop culture moves on. Will's timing with 'last week' is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: The kids got sick of her.
jokes.setup: Jerry's elaborate explanation of music industry terminology
jokes.punchline: Simple, blunt translation of what all the jargon meant
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of industry speak into plain English. The contrast between complex jargon and simple reality is effective.

jokes.text: How much is 15 percent of nothing? / It's... Okay, 15. Hold up, 15, carry the zero. Fifteen... / Nothing, you math whiz.
jokes.setup: Ashley asking about Will's percentage of her failing career
jokes.punchline: Will's elaborate attempt to calculate 15% of nothing, Ashley's sarcastic conclusion
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment with Will's fake calculation, shows both characters coming to terms with reality.

jokes.text: Well, it's nice to know my family was rooting for me to fail.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil and Will expressing happiness about Ashley's career cooling off
jokes.punchline: Ashley's sarcastic observation about lack of family support
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that shows Ashley's awareness and hurt feelings. Sets up the emotional reconciliation.

jokes.text: Or if not, we can use it to prop up an unlevel table. You know, so it don't wobble.
jokes.setup: Will suggesting Ashley's signed CD might be worth money someday
jokes.punchline: Alternative practical use as furniture leveling
jokes.explanation: Standard 'your CD is worthless' joke. The explanation about wobbling is unnecessary and hurts the economy.

jokes.text: No, cold-and-flu season.
jokes.setup: Ashley asking if Will is crying during their emotional reconciliation
jokes.punchline: Will's macho denial using seasonal allergies as excuse
jokes.explanation: Classic Will move - too cool to admit emotional vulnerability. Good character consistency but predictable joke.
